skill.name: clinicaltrials-gov-parser
skill.description: Monitor and summarize competitor clinical trial status changes from ClinicalTrials.gov.
skill.summary: Automated monitoring of clinical trial status changes and competitive intelligence from the ClinicalTrials.gov public database.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Security review of clinicaltrials-gov-parser (community skill). All 144 static findings were evaluated in context: 137 are false positives in documentation/reference files; remaining risk factors (external_commands, network, filesystem) are inherent to the skill's legitimate function of querying the ClinicalTrials.gov public API and executing Python scripts. No evidence of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, data theft, or prompt injection was found. The skill is safe to publish with appropriate risk documentation.

content.user_title: Track and analyze competitor clinical trial data
content.value_statement: Researchers need to track competitor clinical trial status changes across multiple studies. This skill automates monitoring of ClinicalTrials.gov data, providing structured reports on trial status, recruitment, and phase changes.
actual_capabilities: Search for clinical trials by sponsor, condition, status, and phase
Get detailed trial information using NCT identifiers
Monitor status changes across multiple trials over time
Generate summary reports with status breakdowns and recent activity
Track recruitment and enrollment progress for specific studies
Export trial data in JSON or table format
limitations: Requires internet access to query the ClinicalTrials.gov public API
Subject to ClinicalTrials.gov API rate limits of 10 requests per second
Does not support authentication-protected or non-public trial data
Data quality depends on sponsor submissions to the ClinicalTrials.gov registry

use_cases.title: Competitive Intelligence for Pharma Researchers
use_cases.description: Monitor competitor clinical trial activities including new recruiting studies, status changes, and phase transitions to inform strategic decisions.

use_cases.title: Clinical Trial Portfolio Tracking
use_cases.description: Track a portfolio of trials across multiple sponsors and conditions with automated status change detection and summary reporting.

use_cases.title: Recruitment Monitoring for Site Managers
use_cases.description: Track enrollment progress and recruitment status changes for active clinical trials at specific sites or therapy areas.
use_cases.target_user: Clinical site managers and recruitment coordinators

prompt_templates.title: Search for competitor trials
prompt_templates.prompt: Search ClinicalTrials.gov for [sponsor/condition] trials in [status] phase. List all matching trials with their NCT IDs, current status, and phase.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor specific trials for changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the latest status of these clinical trials: [NCT IDs]. Report any status changes compared to the previous state and highlight high-priority transitions like SUSPENDED or TERMINATED.

prompt_templates.title: Generate competitive landscape report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a competitive intelligence report for [sponsor name] in [condition area]. Include total trials, status breakdown, phase distribution, and any trials with recent status changes in the last [N] days.

prompt_templates.title: Track enrollment across a trial portfolio
prompt_templates.prompt: Track enrollment progress for all active [sponsor] trials in [condition]. Identify trials nearing full enrollment, those with slow recruitment, and any trials that have changed status in the past [N] days.

output_examples.input: Search for Pfizer diabetes trials that are currently recruiting
output_examples.output: Found 15 Pfizer diabetes trials currently recruiting. Top results include: NCT05108922 (Phase 3 - GLP-1 Receptor Agonist), NCT04848220 (Phase 2 - Insulin Combination), and NCT04567890 (Phase 3 - SGLT2 Inhibitor). Each trial is actively enrolling participants across multiple sites.

output_examples.input: Check for status changes in my monitored trials this week
output_examples.output: Status changes detected in the past 7 days. 2 trials were SUSPENDED (safety concern alert), 3 trials transitioned to COMPLETED (results pending), and 1 trial terminated early (NCT05108923 - business decision). 5 new RECRUITING trials were added to the competitive landscape.

output_examples.input: Generate a competitive report for Roche oncology trials
output_examples.output: Roche Oncology Portfolio Summary: 47 total trials tracked. Status breakdown: 22 RECRUITING, 12 ACTIVE_NOT_RECRUITING, 8 COMPLETED, 3 SUSPENDED, 2 TERMINATED. Phase distribution: 10 Phase 1, 18 Phase 2, 15 Phase 3, 4 Phase 4. Recent updates: 3 trials with status changes in the last 30 days.

best_practices: Always specify sponsor, condition, and status filters to narrow search results and avoid hitting API rate limits.
Use specific NCT IDs for precise monitoring rather than broad searches to get targeted status change data.
Set an appropriate monitoring window of 7 to 30 days based on how frequently trial statuses change in your therapy area.
anti_patterns: Do not search without filters as this returns too many results and wastes API quota.
Do not make rapid consecutive API calls without respecting the rate limit delay between requests.
Do not rely on a single data pull for critical decisions; cross-reference results with ClinicalTrials.gov directly.

faq.question: What data source does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill uses the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2, the US government database of clinical trials worldwide.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The ClinicalTrials.gov API is publicly accessible and does not require authentication.

faq.question: What is the rate limit for API requests?
faq.answer: The API allows up to 10 requests per second. The skill automatically enforces a delay between requests to stay within limits.

faq.question: Can I track trials from any sponsor?
faq.answer: Yes. You can search for trials by any sponsor, condition, status, or phase. The database covers trials registered globally.

faq.question: What output formats are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports JSON and table formats for search results and monitoring data.

faq.question: How current is the clinical trial data?
faq.answer: Data comes directly from ClinicalTrials.gov in real-time. Trial status accuracy depends on when sponsors submit updates to the registry.
